Felt like it was a closer game than the score would imply, although when you think about it, the Netherlands perfectly exploited Sweden’s two low quality defenders (Lagerbielke and Hien). The goals were not ”fluky” at all, and the win with the final score seems quite fair given that Sweden does not have better defenders than that

Then it seems like they switched something around after the first water break, did a lot better than switched back after half time and conceded again. Weird.

Like most European managers, Koeman underestimated the sheer grit of Japan and paid the price. There’s a reason why Japan are always crowd favourites - despite being physically outmatched, they will eat the grass before giving up.

It's happened. Denmark didn't qualify for Euro 1992, but they got invited as qualifier runners-up because of the war in former Yugoslavia. They ended up winning the tournament.
